children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: a Guidelines
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Task Force shall, after public notice and opportunity for comment, issue guidelines for the use, maintenance, and oversight of environmental banks in Louisiana.December 16, 2016

set forth procedures for establishment and approval of environmental banks subject to the approval of the heads of the appropriate Federal agencies responsible for implementation of Federal environmental laws for which mitigation credits may be used;

establish criteria for siting of environmental banks that enhance the resilience of coastal resources to inundation and coastal erosion in high priority areas, as identified within Federal or State restoration plans, including the restoration of resources within the scope of a project authorized for construction;

establish criteria that ensure environmental banks secure adequate financial assurances and legally enforceable protection for the land or resources that generate the credits from environmental banks;

stipulate that credits from environmental banks may not be used for mitigation of impacts required under section 404 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act () or the Endangered Species Act ( et seq.) in an area where an existing mitigation bank approved pursuant to such laws within 5 years of enactment of the Water Resources Development Act of 2016 has credits available;33 U.S.C. 1342116 U.S.C. 1531See References in Text note below.1
establish performance criteria for environmental banks; and
establish criteria and financial assurance for the operation and monitoring of environmental banks.

children.heading: Definition of environmental bank
children.label: 1 Definition of environmental bank
children.text: In this section, the term “environmental bank” means a project, project increment, or projects for purposes of restoring, creating, or enhancing natural resources at a designated site to establish mitigation credits.

children.heading: Credits
children.label: 2 Credits
children.text: Mitigation credits created from environmental banks approved pursuant to this section may be used to satisfy existing liability under Federal environmental laws.

children.label: A
children.text: the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ( et seq.);33 U.S.C. 1251

children.label: B
children.text: the Endangered Species Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 1531

children.label: C
children.text: the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 ( et seq.);33 U.S.C. 2701

children.label: D
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: A
children.text: any authority, regulatory determination, or legal obligation in effect the day before ; orDecember 16, 2016

children.label: B
children.text: the obligations or requirements of any Federal environmental law.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: e Sunset
children.text: No new environmental bank may be created or approved pursuant to this section after the date that is 14 years after .December 16, 2016
